subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How Bruises Form
description: A quick, practical guide to How Bruises Form.

hook.text: A bruise changes color like a sunset: red, blue, purple, green, yellow. Each color tells you its age.

buildup.text: Impact ruptures tiny blood vessels called capillaries, leaking blood into surrounding tissue.

discovery.text: Hemoglobin in the leaked blood breaks down through chemical stages—each stage has a different color.

twist.text: Easy bruising can signal blood disorders, vitamin deficiencies, or medication side effects.

climax.text: Bruises that appear without impact or in unusual locations should always be evaluated by a doctor.

punchline.text: Red to blue to green to yellow. Your body painting a timeline of its own repair.

quiz.question: What causes the changing colors of a bruise?
options: Hemoglobin breaking down through different chemical stages
Different types of blood cells dying
